g.text: CONSUMER: Future Parser (parser.py -- NOT YET BUILT)
ORANGE border| token stream channel (the contract)
GRAY dashed | future parser (not yet built)
type | TokenType | KEYWORD / NUMBER / EOF / ERROR ...
lexeme| str | exact source text e.g. ">="
ORDER | tokens emitted left-to-right, line order
EOF | last token is ALWAYS TokenType.EOF
ONE EOF | exactly one EOF sentinel per scan
IMMUT | list is complete before parser starts
NO SRC | parser never touches source string
annotation-1 | EOF is stream terminator -- parser loops until EOF
annotation-2 | ERROR tokens flow through -- parser handles or skips
annotation-3 | positions are SNAPSHOT at token START (not end)
annotation-4 | whitespace + comments ABSENT -- consumed silently
annotation-5 | keyword vs identifier resolved HERE -- parser sees KEYWORD
RULE | parser.advance() consumes Token not char
RULE | scanner.advance() consumes char not Token
RULE | scanner is DONE before parser starts
RULE | token.lexeme is the only src text visible
RULE | token.line+col used for all diagnostics
Scanner emits ERROR token into list[Token]
Parser receives ERROR -- treats as unknown
Parser may emit additional parse errors
Pro: parser gets location of bad char
Con: parser must guard every rule for ERROR
Con: two output channels to thread through
while not self.is_at_end(): # EOF is terminator
